name: Morella: A Star Wars Story
description:
Morella was born on the icy planet of Csilla, homeworld of the Chiss species, into a minor yet respected family within the Chiss Ascendancy. Her lineage, while not part of the Ruling Families, held influence in the spheres of diplomacy and military coordination. Raised in a household that valued discipline, honor, and strategic intellect, Morella was destined from an early age to follow in her mother’s footsteps as a tactician for one of the Expansionary Defense Fleet’s auxiliary branches.

Growing up, she excelled in formal education, displaying a sharp mind for logistics, starship systems, and interstellar politics. She spent her formative years moving between diplomatic summits, tactical simulations, and long nights buried in data pads. While her life was rigid and structured, she found solace in quiet stargazing, always wondering what lay beyond Chiss space and the reach of Ascendancy doctrine.

The Fall from Grace

However, her world unraveled when a political rival of her family accused her father of leaking sensitive information to outsiders—a claim that was manufactured, but effective. In Chiss society, such allegations are catastrophic. Overnight, her family lost its standing, assets were seized, and they were rounded up and executed.

Morella escaped however and first fled to Hutt Space, one of the few regions that cared little for Chiss politics. Penniless, hunted by those who wanted to silence her permanently, and lost in a galaxy she only studied from behind the safety of Ascendancy walls, she quickly discovered that her talents held little value in the underworld. She was truly alone.

The Gutter and the Glow

After leaving Hutt Space, she then arrived on Morlana One with nothing—no credits, no allies, no identity anyone respected. Out of options and desperate to survive, she accepted work at a sleazy entertainment den, first as a server, then later as a dancer. It wasn’t long before she was pressured into performing more degrading services such as stripping, escorting, prostitution, pornography. At first, she resisted. Then the choice became starvation or submission.

To protect what little agency she had left, Morella created an online persona through a holo platform called OnlyStars (Star Wars version of OnlyFans), where she could at least control what she shared and when. It gave her enough to afford cheap shelter at the brothel, the occasional warm meal, and most importantly, a savings account she buried under layers of false identities and code.

Though she's hardened on the outside, Morella never let go of her past entirely. Her mind remains sharp, her observational skills precise. She still reads galactic trade reports in her spare time, practices her Chiss speech patterns to stay connected to her culture, and runs flight simulations late at night on borrowed terminals.

Dreams in the Dark

Each week she tells herself it’s the last one—that soon she’ll have enough credits to buy passage to a new system, take on freelance starship work, or just disappear among the stars. She dreams of owning a small ship of her own, flying far from Morlana One to the edges of the galaxy, living quietly among starlight and silence.

But the brothel keeps calling her back. Her face and body are known there. The credits are fast. And escaping feels harder with every step.

She’s come close to leaving more than once. Once, she made it to the spaceport before being caught by one of the brothel’s hired thugs and dragged back under threat of exposure. Another time, her credits were stolen by a client she trusted. Her most recent escape attempt ended with her leg injured in an alley fight—one she wouldn’t have survived if a stranger hadn’t stepped in.

A Spark Left Burning

Now, with an injured leg and a new friend by her side that she hasn’t had in ages, Morella stares at a flickering star map on the wall. Each dot is a place she’s never been. Each one a future she still believes is po
creator: TheCallsignX
